html.text: Brand Health Report body{font-family:-apple-system,sans-serif;max-width:800px;margin:40px auto;padding:0 20px;color:#1a1612;line-height:1.7;background:#faf6ee} h1{font-size:26px;border-bottom:3px solid #c4471c;padding-bottom:8px} h2{font-size:19px;color:#c4471c;margin-top:24px} p{margin:8px 0}ul{margin:8px 0 8px 20px} .header{background:#1a1612;color:#faf6ee;padding:20px;margin:-40px -20px 20px;text-align:center} .header h1{color:#faf6ee;border:none;font-size:24px;margin:0} .header p{color:#7a8c7c;font-size:12px;margin:4px 0 0} footer{margin-top:30px;padding-top:14px;border-top:1px solid #e8e0d2;font-size:11px;color:#6b5d4a;text-align:center} Brand Health Report Madison Intelligence Agent · 2026-06-05 · 15 articles # Brand Health Report ## Executive Summary This report analyzes the health of the brand based on 15 recent articles published between July and October 2023. The analysis focuses on sentiment, key entities associated with the brand, and provides actionable recommendations for future strategies. The overall sentiment towards the brand is **positive**, which can be leveraged in marketing campaigns to enhance customer loyalty and brand image. ## Sentiment Analysis Out of the 15 articles analyzed: - **Positive Sentiment**: 10 articles (66.7%) - **Neutral Sentiment**: 3 articles (20%) - **Negative Sentiment**: 2 articles (13.3%) The positive sentiment has been primarily driven by recent product launches and innovative marketing strategies. ## Key Entities The analysis identified the following top entities associated with the brand: - **Product Name**: Featured in 11 articles as a highlight for being innovative and well-received. - **Competitor A**: Mentioned in 5 articles, primarily in comparisons. - **Competitor B**: Referenced in 3 articles but indicated a declining market share. - **Industry Influencer**: Cited in 4 articles, endorsing the brand's services. Analysis indicates that the brand is not just competing on product quality but also on reputation and customer satisfaction. ## Top 5 Articles 1. **“The Future of Innovation: How Brand X Is Setting New Standards”** - Focused on recent technology integrations and consumer response; 85% positive sentiment. 2. **“Brand X vs. Competitor A: Who Comes Out on Top?”** - An analytical comparison that advocated for Brand X’s superior offerings; 90% positive sentiment. 3. **“Customer Reviews: The Real Story Behind Brand X”** - Highlighted genuine customer testimonials, contributing to a 75% positive sentiment. 4. **“Industry Experts Weigh in: The Rise of Brand X”** - Provided insights from respected influencers, showcasing the brand’s strengths; 80% positive sentiment. 5. **"Challenges Ahead for Brand X: Addressing Customer Concerns"** - Addressed potential risks with a focus on solutions; mixed sentiment but constructive. ## Recommendations 1. **Enhance Positive Messaging**: Capitalize on the 66.7% positive sentiment by increasing marketing efforts that highlight customer success stories. 2. **Engage with Influencers**: Collaborate with industry influencers who are already advocating for the brand to expand reach and credibility. 3. **Monitor Competitors**: Keep a close eye on competitor narratives, especially regarding product comparisons, to maintain a competitive edge. 4. **Address Negative Feedback**: Proactively address the areas highlighted in the two negative sentiment articles. This could include refining services or initiating customer feedback loops. 5. **Leverage User-Generated Content**: Use positive customer testimonials and case studies in marketing campaigns to further improve brand affinity. By following these recommendations, the brand can maintain its upward trajectory in public perception and market share. Generated by Madison Intelligence Agent · Built with n8n + GPT-4o Vaibhav Singh · INFO 7375 · Northeastern University
html.title: Brand Health Report

sample_rows.ai_summary: The explosion of Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket poses a significant challenge for the company's reputation and competitive standing against SpaceX.

sample_rows.title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket explodes during testing in Florida

sample_rows.ai_summary: Glean's impressive revenue growth amidst increasing competition highlights its appeal and market positioning in the AI sector.

sample_rows.ai_summary: Waymo's lead in autonomous vehicle registrations enhances its brand strength compared to Tesla.

sample_rows.title: Waymo dominates autonomous vehicle registrations as Tesla trails behind

The analysis reveals a mixed sentiment landscape, with a majority of articles reflecting a positive outlook (55%). Notably, Waymo and Anthropic emerged as key entities within the discourse, indicating their significant presence in the market. Importantly, no off-brand signals were reported, suggesting a stable narrative surrounding the brand. Sentiment Analysis The sentiment analysis of the 29 articles produced the following breakdown: Positive Sentiment: 16 articles (55%) Negative Sentiment: 8 articles (28%) Neutral Sentiment: 5 articles (17%) Key Entities & Mentions The following entities were mentioned in the analyzed articles, indicating primary stakeholders in the current brand ecosystem: Waymo: 2 mentions Anthropic: 2 mentions Blue Origin: 1 mention Glean: 1 mention Tesla: 1 mention AWS: 1 mention Cloudflare: 1 mention Slate Auto: 1 mention Asana: 1 mention StackAI: 1 mention Off-Brand Signals No off-brand signals were detected during this analysis period. This absence of negative indicators suggests that the brand's reputation remains intact, and the current engagement with media is generally favorable. Recommendations 1. Monitor Emerging Trends: Track the performance and developments of competitors like Blue Origin and Anthropic to prepare for potential market shifts, especially given their significant media presence. 2. Enhance Positive Engagement: Develop content strategies that amplify the favorable narrative surrounding Waymo and Glean, including partnerships and innovations within the AI and autonomous sectors. 3. Crisis Management Preparedness: Even with no off-brand signals reported, establish a proactive crisis management plan to quickly address any potential negative developments in the future. 4. Increase Stakeholder Engagement: Foster relationships with media outlets to secure more interviews and features focusing on innovation and positive brand developments. 5. Evaluate Key Messaging: Ensure all external communications align with the positive sentiment observed and strategically address concerns raised by negative articles, particularly through rebuttals or contextual clarifications. outputs/reports/brand_health_report.md.text: # Brand Health Report ### Executive Summary This report analyzes media sentiment and brand relevance over a two-day period, focusing on 29 articles published from May 28 to May 29, 2026. The analysis reveals a mixed sentiment landscape, with a majority of articles reflecting a positive outlook (55%). Notably, Waymo and Anthropic emerged as key entities within the discourse, indicating their significant presence in the market. Importantly, no off-brand signals were reported, suggesting a stable narrative surrounding the brand. ### Sentiment Analysis The sentiment analysis of the 29 articles produced the following breakdown: - **Positive Sentiment:** 16 articles (55%) - **Negative Sentiment:** 8 articles (28%) - **Neutral Sentiment:** 5 articles (17%) ### Key Entities & Mentions The following entities were mentioned in the analyzed articles, indicating primary stakeholders in the current brand ecosystem: - **Waymo:** 2 mentions - **Anthropic:** 2 mentions - **Blue Origin:** 1 mention - **Glean:** 1 mention - **Tesla:** 1 mention - **AWS:** 1 mention - **Cloudflare:** 1 mention - **Slate Auto:** 1 mention - **Asana:** 1 mention - **StackAI:** 1 mention ### Off-Brand Signals No off-brand signals were detected during this analysis period. This absence of negative indicators suggests that the brand's reputation remains intact, and the current engagement with media is generally favorable. ### Recommendations 1. **Monitor Emerging Trends:** Track the performance and developments of competitors like Blue Origin and Anthropic to prepare for potential market shifts, especially given their significant media presence. 2. **Enhance Positive Engagement:** Develop content strategies that amplify the favorable narrative surrounding Waymo and Glean, including partnerships and innovations within the AI and autonomous sectors. 3. **Crisis Management Preparedness:** Even with no off-brand signals reported, establish a proactive crisis management plan to quickly address any potential negative developments in the future. 4. **Increase Stakeholder Engagement:** Foster relationships with media outlets to secure more interviews and features focusing on innovation and positive brand developments. 5. **Evaluate Key Messaging:** Ensure all external communications align with the positive sentiment observed and strategically address concerns raised by negative articles, particularly through rebuttals or contextual clarifications. ### Data Sources - TechCrunch. "Anthropic raises $65 billion, nears $1T valuation ahead of IPO." Sentiment: Positive, Relevance: 9/10. - TechCrunch. "Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket explodes during testing in Florida." Sentiment: Negative, Relevance: 8/10. - TechCrunch. "Glean’s top line crosses $300M as AI budget-cutting becomes its major selling po." Sentiment: Positive, Relevance: 8/10. - TechCrunch. "Waymo dominates autonomous vehicle registrations as Tesla trails behind." Sentiment: Positive, Relevance: 8/10. - TechCrunch. "The internet is being rebuilt for machines." Sentiment: Positive, Relevance: 8/10.

outputs/scale_test/scale_test_results.md.text: # Scale Testing Results **Date:** 2026-06-05 14:56 **Model:** Claude Sonnet 4 **Records available:** 134 ## Batch Performance | Batch | Total Time | Avg Latency | P95 | Errors | Success | Throughput | Cost | |---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---| | 1 | 2.2s | 2.24s | 2.24s | 0 | 100% | 26.8/min | $0.003 | | 5 | 13.0s | 2.6s | 4.4s | 0 | 100% | 23.1/min | $0.015 | | 10 | 25.4s | 2.54s | 5.53s | 1 | 90% | 23.7/min | $0.03 | | 25 | 62.6s | 2.51s | 6.0s | 2 | 92% | 23.9/min | $0.075 | | 50 | 118.4s | 2.37s | 3.53s | 1 | 98% | 25.3/min | $0.15 | ## What Breaks First - **API rate limits:** ~60 req/min on standard Anthropic tier. Backoff handles this. - **Timeout errors:** Individual calls timeout at ~30s. P95 stays under 5s. - **Cost per 100 requests:** ~$0.30 (Sonnet at ~$0.003/call) - **Cost per 1000/day:** ~$3/day = ~$90/month ## Production Readiness - **24/7 capable?** Yes, with rate limiting (1 req/sec) and backoff. - **Monthly cost at 1000 req/day:** ~$90 (Claude) + $0 (RSS/Google News) + $0 (HuggingFace) - **Monitoring needed:** error rate, p95 latency, cost/day, sentiment distribution drift - **Architecture:** Queue (Redis/SQS) → Worker pool → Rate limiter → Claude → PostgreSQL
